title: Poems of Passion
summaries: "Poems of Passion" by Ella Wheeler Wilcox is a poetry collection published in 1883. Despite its provocative title threatening scandal, the book found widespread acceptance among respectable midwestern readers. The collection explores themes of love, longing, friendship, and human emotion through dozens of poems. From passionate declarations to philosophical reflections on art, courage, and solitude, Wilcox's verses capture the complexity of romantic and spiritual experience in nineteenth-century America.
